message:
fix(daemon): honor registered semantic embedders

Thread daemon embedding jobs through the requested FastEmbedder model instead of treating every semantic worker pass as MiniLM.

Bead: coding_agent_session_search-klnxl

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-khh6","title":"Task 4.5: Migrate Existing renderer.rs and template.rs Tests","description":"# Objective
Update all existing tests in renderer.rs and template.rs to work with the new MessageGroup API.

## Scope
- 19 tests in renderer.rs
- 10 tests in template.rs
- Total: 29 tests need migration

## Migration Strategy

### Tests That Test render_message (keep, adapt)
These test individual message rendering - can be adapted to test rendering within a group:
- test_render_message_user
- test_render_message_with_code
- test_html_escape_in_content
- test_message_with_index
- test_message_with_author
- test_long_message_collapse

Strategy: Create MessageGroup wrapper, test that primary message renders correctly.

### Tests That Test render_conversation (must change)
These will change from Vec<Message> to Vec<MessageGroup>:
- test_conversation_with_agent_class

Strategy: Create multiple MessageGroups, verify agent class applied.

### Tests That Test Tool Rendering (significant changes)
- test_tool_status_rendering
- test_tool_icons_for_different_tools

Strategy: Update to test badges in header, not separate messages.

### Tests That Stay Same (helper functions)
- test_render_inline_code
- test_render_links
- test_url_with_query_params_not_double_escaped
- test_agent_css_class
- test_agent_display_name
- test_format_json_or_raw
- test_truncate_to_char_boundary
- test_long_message_collapse_utf8_safe
- test_tool_output_truncation_utf8_safe
- test_format_timestamp_utf8_safe

These test pure functions that don't depend on Message/MessageGroup.

### template.rs Tests
- test_export_messages_plain → update to use MessageGroup
- test_export_logs_include_milestones → update messages
- test_export_messages_requires_password_when_encrypted → update
- test_export_messages_encrypted_payload → update

## Test Helpers to Create

```rust
/// Create a simple user MessageGroup
fn test_user_group(content: &str) -> MessageGroup {
    MessageGroup::new(
        Message {
            role: "user".to_string(),
            content: content.to_string(),
            timestamp: None,
            tool_call: None,
            index: None,
            author: None,
        },
        MessageGroupType::User,
    )
}

/// Create an assistant MessageGroup with tool calls
fn test_assistant_group_with_tools(
    content: &str,
    tools: Vec<(&str, &str, ToolStatus)>
) -> MessageGroup {
    let mut group = MessageGroup::new(...);
    for (name, input, status) in tools {
        group.add_tool_call(...);
    }
    group
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 19 renderer.rs tests pass
- [ ] All 10 template.rs tests pass
- [ ] New helper functions for creating test groups
- [ ] No test coverage regression
- [ ] cargo test passes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T22:12:53.287160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:28:45.398826Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T23:28:45.398748Z","close_reason":"Complete - All 51 renderer/template tests pass with MessageGroup API. Factory methods (user/assistant/system/tool_only) provide clean test helpers.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-khh6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1nk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-khh6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2jxn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-ki5jc","title":"Document disabled search_sqlite_fts5() stub at its call site","description":"## What

Add documentation at the call site of search_sqlite_fts5() and a test locking in the disabled behavior.

## Current State

- Function definition (line 4532): Well-documented as intentionally disabled
- Call site (line ~2797): No comment explaining the intentional empty-return behavior

## Fix — 2 changes

### 1. Add comment at call site (~line 2797)

```rust
// NOTE: FTS5 path intentionally disabled (returns empty); see search_sqlite_fts5() doc.
let hits = self.search_sqlite_fts5(db_path, query, filters.clone(), ...);
```

### 2. Add behavioral test in \`#[cfg(test)] mod tests\` (line 5465+)

The test constructs a minimal SearchClient inline (no helper function exists — all tests in query.rs construct SearchClient directly). The constructor must match the current struct shape at implementation time.

```rust
#[test]
fn search_sqlite_fts5_returns_empty_by_design() {
    // search_sqlite_fts5() is intentionally disabled: it always returns Ok(vec![]).
    // The primary lexical engine is Tantivy via frankensearch. The FTS5 path is
    // retained to exercise query transpilation and as a hook for future
    // frankensqlite FTS5 support.
    //
    // If this test fails (returns non-empty), it means FTS5 support was
    // implemented — update the call site comment and this test accordingly.

    // Construct minimal SearchClient (match current struct fields at impl time)
    let client = SearchClient {
        reader: None,
        sqlite: Mutex::new(None),
        sqlite_path: None,
        prefix_cache: Mutex::new(CacheShards::new(*CACHE_TOTAL_CAP, *CACHE_BYTE_CAP)),
        reload_on_search: false,
        last_reload: Mutex::new(None),
        last_generation: Mutex::new(None),
        reload_epoch: Arc::new(AtomicU64::new(0)),
        warm_tx: None,
        _warm_handle: None,
        // NOTE: If ix93s has been completed, omit the _shared_filters line below.
        // If ix93s has NOT been completed yet, include it:
        // _shared_filters: Arc::new(Mutex::new(())),
        metrics: Metrics::default(),
        cache_namespace: \"test\".to_string(),
        semantic: Mutex::new(None),
        last_tantivy_total_count: Mutex::new(None),
    };

    let hits = client.search_sqlite_fts5(
        Path::new(\"/nonexistent\"),
        \"test query\",
        SearchFilters::default(),
        10,
        0,
        FieldMask::default(),
    );
    assert!(hits.is_ok(), \"FTS5 stub must not return error\");
    assert!(
        hits.unwrap().is_empty(),
        \"FTS5 path must return empty vec until frankensqlite gains FTS5 support\"
    );
}
```

**Implementer note:** Check whether bead ix93s has already removed the _shared_filters field. If yes, omit that line from the constructor. If no, include it. The verification gate (asen9) will catch any struct mismatch.

## Verification

- Comment exists at call site (~line 2797)
- cargo test search_sqlite_fts5 -- --nocapture passes
- No functional changes introduced","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:17:42.051340254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:28:25.171857577Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:28:25.171564869Z","close_reason":"Already completed by concurrent agent: call site comment added at lines 2790-2791, search_sqlite_fts5_returns_empty_by_design test added at line 11371. Verified in code.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","search"]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-klnxl","title":"Daemon embedding worker rejects registered semantic embedders","description":"mock-code-finder genuine finding: src/daemon/worker.rs has a placeholder-like hardcoded semantic model gate that accepts only minilm/fastembed, returning 'unsupported semantic model' for registered FastEmbedder models such as snowflake-arctic-s and nomic-embed. The registry, manifests, and FastEmbedder::load_by_name already support those names, so daemon background embedding jobs fail for valid configured semantic embedders instead of loading the requested model. Fix by threading the requested model name through the daemon FastEmbed path and adding unit coverage for registered model aliases.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T17:03:00.858720011Z","created_by":"IvorySummit","updated_at":"2026-04-24T17:03:05.564613404Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-klnxl","title":"Daemon embedding worker rejects registered semantic embedders","description":"mock-code-finder genuine finding: src/daemon/worker.rs has a placeholder-like hardcoded semantic model gate that accepts only minilm/fastembed, returning 'unsupported semantic model' for registered FastEmbedder models such as snowflake-arctic-s and nomic-embed. The registry, manifests, and FastEmbedder::load_by_name already support those names, so daemon background embedding jobs fail for valid configured semantic embedders instead of loading the requested model. Fix by threading the requested model name through the daemon FastEmbed path and adding unit coverage for registered model aliases.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T17:03:00.858720011Z","created_by":"IvorySummit","updated_at":"2026-04-24T17:11:23.756956071Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T17:11:23.756536375Z","close_reason":"Fixed daemon embedding worker to resolve and load registered FastEmbedder models instead of hardcoding MiniLM; added daemon alias coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-klyc","title":"[PROFILE] Verify Profiling Data Before Implementation","description":"## Overview (from PLAN Section 1.B and Section 3)

Before implementing optimizations, verify the profiling data is current and accurate. Stale profiles can lead to optimizing the wrong hotspots.

## Profiling Verification Steps

### 1. CPU Profiling with perf (Section 3.1)

**Indexing hotspots** to verify:
```bash
RUSTFLAGS="-C force-frame-pointers=yes" cargo build --profile profiling
perf record -F 99 -g ./target/profiling/cass index --full
perf report --sort=dso,symbol
```

Expected hotspots (verify these still dominate):
- 2.73% `tantivy_stacker::expull::ExpUnrolledLinkedListWriter::write_u32_vint`
- 2.36% `tantivy::tokenizer::simple_tokenizer::SimpleTokenStream::advance`
- 2.20% `core::str::iter::CharIndices::next`
- 1.19% `coding_agent_search::search::tantivy::generate_edge_ngrams`
- 1.13% `sqlite3VdbeExec`

**Search hotspots** to verify:
- 3.63% `[kernel] clear_page_erms` (page faults / cold-open)
- 3.44% `tantivy::store::reader::StoreReader::read_block` (stored field reads)
- 1.16% `tantivy_fst::regex::dfa::Dfa::add`
- 0.86% `tantivy::query::regex_query::RegexQuery::from_pattern`

### 2. I/O Profiling with strace (Section 3.2)

**Indexing syscalls** (should match these patterns):
```bash
strace -c ./target/release/cass index --full 2>&1
```
Expected:
- `futex`: ~22,689
- `pwrite64`: ~31,443
- `pread64`: ~9,109
- `openat`: ~3,330
- `fdatasync`: ~194

**Search syscalls** (200 runs of substring wildcard):
Expected per-run:
- `openat`: ~121
- `mmap`: ~340
- `munmap`: ~242

### 3. Allocation Profiling (Section 3.3)

Using jemalloc profiling:
```bash
MALLOC_CONF=prof:true,prof_prefix:jeprof ./target/release/cass index --full
jeprof --svg ./target/release/cass jeprof.*.heap > alloc_profile.svg
```

Expected total allocation for 36k messages: ~1,375 MB
Major buckets:
- Rust vec growth
- SQLite allocation
- Edge-ngrams generation

## Validation Checklist

- [ ] CPU profile matches expected hotspots (within 20%)
- [ ] I/O profile matches expected syscall patterns
- [ ] Allocation profile identifies same buckets
- [ ] No new unexpected hotspots have emerged
- [ ] Profiling data is from current codebase version

## If Profiles Don't Match

If profiles have changed significantly:
1. Re-run baseline benchmarks
2. Update PLAN document with new data
3. Re-evaluate optimization priorities
4. Create new beads if needed

## Dependencies
- Should run after: coding_agent_session_search-8uw2 (baseline recording)

Baseline recorded in docs/perf/baseline_round1.md.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-klyc","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-8uw2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-km9j","title":"P1.4: Basic CLI Interface (--export-only)","description":"# Basic CLI Interface (--export-only)

**Parent Phase:** coding_agent_session_search-6uo3 (Phase 1: Core Export)
**Estimated Duration:** 2-3 days

## Goal

Implement the minimal CLI interface for `cass pages --export-only` that allows testing the export pipeline without the full wizard or encryption.

## Technical Approach

### CLI Command Structure

```
cass pages --export-only <DIR> [OPTIONS]

OPTIONS:
    Content Selection:
        --agents <LIST>       Comma-separated agent slugs [default: all]
        --workspaces <LIST>   Comma-separated workspace paths [default: all]
        --since <DATE>        Include conversations after date
        --until <DATE>        Include conversations before date

    Privacy Controls:
        --path-mode <MODE>    How to store paths: relative|basename|full|hash
        --stealth             Alias for --path-mode hash (also strips metadata)

    Output:
        --dry-run             Show what would be exported, don't export
        --json                Output progress as JSON (for automation)
        --verbose             Show detailed progress

EXIT CODES:
    0   Success
    1   General error
    2   Invalid arguments
    5   User cancelled
```

### Implementation in `src/main.rs` or `src/cli/pages.rs`

```rust
#[derive(Parser)]
pub struct PagesCommand {
    #[command(subcommand)]
    pub action: Option<PagesAction>,

    /// Export to directory without deployment
    #[arg(long)]
    pub export_only: Option<PathBuf>,

    /// Agents to include (comma-separated)
    #[arg(long, value_delimiter = ',')]
    pub agents: Option<Vec<String>>,

    /// Workspaces to include (comma-separated)
    #[arg(long, value_delimiter = ',')]
    pub workspaces: Option<Vec<PathBuf>>,

    /// Include conversations after this date
    #[arg(long)]
    pub since: Option<String>,  // Parse as date

    /// Include conversations before this date
    #[arg(long)]
    pub until: Option<String>,

    /// Path storage mode
    #[arg(long, default_value = "relative")]
    pub path_mode: PathMode,

    /// Enable stealth mode (hash paths, strip metadata)
    #[arg(long)]
    pub stealth: bool,

    /// Show what would be exported
    #[arg(long)]
    pub dry_run: bool,

    /// Output as JSON
    #[arg(long)]
    pub json: bool,
}
```

### Date Parsing

Support multiple formats:
- ISO 8601: `2024-01-15`
- Relative: `30 days ago`, `last week`, `yesterday`

```rust
fn parse_date(s: &str) -> Result<DateTime<Utc>> {
    // Try ISO 8601 first
    if let Ok(d) = NaiveDate::parse_from_str(s, "%Y-%m-%d") {
        return Ok(d.and_hms_opt(0, 0, 0).unwrap().and_utc());
    }
    // Try relative dates
    parse_relative_date(s)
}
```

### Progress Display (non-JSON mode)

```
$ cass pages --export-only ./output --agents claude-code --since "30 days ago"

Scanning conversations...
  Found: 1,234 conversations, 45,678 messages

Filtering by criteria:
  Agents: claude-code
  Time range: 2024-12-07 to 2025-01-06
  Workspaces: all

After filters:
  Matched: 234 conversations, 8,901 messages

Exporting to ./output/export.sqlite3...
  [████████████████████████████████████████] 234/234 conversations

Generating FTS indexes...
  [████████████████████████████████████████] Complete

Computing statistics...
  [████████████████████████████████████████] Complete

✓ Export complete!
  Output: ./output/export.sqlite3 (12.3 MB)
  Conversations: 234
  Messages: 8,901
```

### JSON Output Mode

```json
{
    "status": "success",
    "output_path": "./output/export.sqlite3",
    "stats": {
        "conversations": 234,
        "messages": 8901,
        "agents": ["claude-code"],
        "time_range": {
            "from": "2024-12-07T00:00:00Z",
            "to": "2025-01-06T23:59:59Z"
        },
        "size_bytes": 12902400
    }
}
```

## Test Cases

1. `--export-only ./out` → exports all data
2. `--agents claude-code` → filters to one agent
3. `--since "30 days ago"` → time filter works
4. `--dry-run` → no files created, stats printed
5. `--json` → valid JSON output
6. Invalid date → helpful error message
7. Non-existent output dir → auto-create with confirmation

## Files to Create/Modify

- `src/cli/mod.rs` (add PagesCommand)
- `src/cli/pages.rs` (new)
- `src/main.rs` (wire up command)
- `tests/cli_pages.rs` (new - integration tests)

## Exit Criteria

1. `cass pages --help` shows all options
2. Export produces valid SQLite file
3. All filter combinations work
4. JSON output is valid
5. Progress bars render correctly in terminal
6. Calls run_pages_export().","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-km9j","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-p4w2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-kmup7","title":"[MEDIUM] parallel_insert_conversation_tree_keeps_unique_external_ids_distinct flaky under concurrent cargo load","description":"FLAKY under multi-agent cargo/rustc load. src/storage/sqlite.rs::tests::parallel_insert_conversation_tree_keeps_unique_external_ids_distinct.

Test uses rayon parallel inserts with a 24-attempt retry loop (with_retry at sqlite.rs:13611) classifying Busy / BusyRecovery / BusySnapshot / WriteConflict / SerializationFailure as retryable. When 6-8 concurrent cargo+rustc processes are active (common in this multi-pane swarm), the 24-retry budget exhausts and the test panics.

EVIDENCE: Failed during full --all-targets run while 6 other cargo procs were active per pstree inspection. Did NOT panic with an assertion; failure mode was retry budget exhaustion.

MITIGATION OPTIONS:
1. Increase retry budget (24 -> 64) when CI is detected.
2. Cap rayon parallelism to 2 threads in the test (currently uses default = num_cpus).
3. Tag #[serial] and acknowledge the test is stress-sensitive.

Option 2 is lowest-risk — the TEST intent is 'concurrent inserts keep external_ids distinct'; 2 threads still exercise that. Owner: storage/indexer pane.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:14:50.440311531Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:46:32.216176957Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:46:32.215820009Z","close_reason":"RESOLVED. Verified 2026-04-23 14:39 UTC: cargo test --lib storage::sqlite::tests::parallel_insert_conversation_tree_keeps_unique_external_ids_distinct passes green across 3 consecutive runs while 5+ other agent panes are running cargo builds — no retry-budget exhaustion observed. Either the 24-retry backoff proved sufficient under current load patterns or a concurrent agent's storage-side commit reduced busy-window duration. No longer reproducing; closing without source change.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flaky"]}
